stem: The base of a solid is the region bounded by the line \( y = x + [[k]] \), the \( x \)-axis, and the vertical lines \( x = [[a]] \) and \( x = [[b]] \). Every cross-section taken perpendicular to the \( x \)-axis is a square. Find the volume of the solid.
solution: At position \( x \) the base has height \( x+[[k]] \), so each square has area \( (x+[[k]])^{2} \). The volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}(x+[[k]])^{2}\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This integrates the side length instead of its square (area).
This ignores the \( +[[k]] \) in the side length.
This doubles the correct volume.
This halves the correct volume, as for triangular cross-sections.
This is off by one from the correct volume.

stem: The base of a solid is the region bounded by the line \( y = x + [[k]] \), the \( x \)-axis, and the vertical lines \( x = [[a]] \) and \( x = [[b]] \). Every cross-section taken perpendicular to the \( x \)-axis is a SEMICIRCLE whose diameter equals the height of the base region. Find the volume of the solid.
solution: A semicircle with diameter \( d=x+[[k]] \) has radius \( d/2 \) and area \( \tfrac{1}{2}\pi(d/2)^{2} = \tfrac{\pi}{8}(x+[[k]])^{2} \). The volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\tfrac{\pi}{8}(x+[[k]])^{2}\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the area of a full circle of diameter \( d \), \( \tfrac{\pi}{4}d^{2}\), doubled by mistake instead of halved.
This scales up as if the cross-section area formula had no \( \tfrac18 \) factor at all.
This drops the factor of \( \pi \).
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by \( \pi \) from the correct volume.

stem: The base of a solid is the region bounded by the line \( y = x + [[k]] \), the \( x \)-axis, and the vertical lines \( x = [[a]] \) and \( x = [[b]] \). Every cross-section taken perpendicular to the \( x \)-axis is an EQUILATERAL TRIANGLE whose side equals the height of the base region. Find the volume of the solid.
solution: An equilateral triangle with side \( s=x+[[k]] \) has area \( \tfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}s^{2} \). The volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\tfrac{\sqrt{3}}{4}(x+[[k]])^{2}\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the area formula for a right triangle with legs \( s \), not an equilateral triangle.
This drops a factor of 2 from the equilateral-triangle area formula.
This forgets the \( \sqrt{3} \) factor entirely.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is off by one from the correct volume.

stem: The base of a solid is the region bounded by the line \( y = x + [[k]] \), the \( x \)-axis, and the vertical lines \( x = [[a]] \) and \( x = [[b]] \). Every cross-section taken perpendicular to the \( x \)-axis is an ISOSCELES RIGHT TRIANGLE whose HYPOTENUSE lies along the base and equals the height of the base region. Find the volume of the solid.
solution: An isosceles right triangle with hypotenuse \( h=x+[[k]] \) has legs \( h/\sqrt{2} \) and area \( \tfrac{1}{2}(h/\sqrt{2})^{2} = \tfrac{h^{2}}{4} \). The volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\tfrac{(x+[[k]])^{2}}{4}\,dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This uses the area formula for a triangle with a LEG (not the hypotenuse) equal to \( h \).
This treats the cross-section as a square of side \( h \), not a triangle.
This doubles the correct volume.
This is four times the correct volume.
This is off by one from the correct volume.

stem: The base of a solid is the region between the curve \( y = x^{2} + [[p]] \) and the line \( y = [[q]]x \), for \( [[a]] \le x \le [[b]] \) (on which the curve lies entirely above the line). Every cross-section taken perpendicular to the \( x \)-axis is a square whose side length equals the vertical distance between the curve and the line. Find the volume of the solid.
solution: At position \( x \) the side length of the square is the gap between the two curves, \( (x^{2}+[[p]]) - [[q]]x \), so its area is \( \left((x^{2}+[[p]]) - [[q]]x\right)^{2} \). The volume is \( \int_{[[a]]}^{[[b]]}\left((x^{2}+[[p]]) - [[q]]x\right)^{2}dx = [[answer]] \).
distractor_rationales: This evaluates the side length only at the endpoints instead of integrating its square over the whole interval.
This uses the side length at \( x=[[b]] \) times the width, a right-rectangle approximation.
This doubles the correct volume.
This halves the correct volume.
This is off by \( [[p]] \) from the correct volume.
